In-silico evaluation of tissue microarray utility in high-grade serous ovarian cancer

Fernando Perez - University of Helsinki

Summer Students Lightning Talks

August 21, 2023

Mentored by: Tuulia Vallius - LSP

Background

Tissue microarrays (TMAs) are a technique to study small tissue sections from several samples on the same slide

Paraffin block

Slide

Background

For high-plex imagining of cohorts, much less data is created with TMAs
Easier to analyze and identify common patterns (biomarkers) in cohorts

Lin et al., 2023, Cell

Paraffin block

Slide

Background

Lin et al., 2023, Cell

TMAs can not recapture bigger structures cellular structures in the tumor microenvironment

Background

Lin et al., 2023, Cell

TMAs can not recapture bigger structures cellular structures in the tumor microenvironment - neither cell proportions

The study questions

Can we use TMA sampling to develop biomarkers using cohorts?
Is the intra-sample variability smaller than inter-sample variability?

Highly dissimilar features in high grade serous ovarian cancer after chemotherapy* exposure

DAPI CK7 ECadherin CD11c CD8 αSMA

Before chemo

After chemo

*Neoadjuvant chemotherapy, platinum based and paclitaxel

Stromal rich

Tumor area

Myeloid rich

The study questions

Can we use TMA sampling to develop biomarkers using cohorts?
Is the intra-sample variability smaller than inter-sample variability?

Is there an strategy for TMA construction that increase concordance to the whole slides?
Are the cores taken from tumor-rich areas or stromal-tumor interface representative of the whole slide?
How many cores needed?

Topacio, 26 samples

22 Finnish (Sciset) samples

48 whole slide 35plex tCyCIF images
Multi-modal data from two independent ovarian cancer cohorts

Study materials

Inga-Maria’s results, in preparation

Neoadjuvant chemotherapy induces CD8.T cell infiltration across different cell neighborhoods

Previous results from the tCycif whole slide images

Conclusions til here

In HGSC, TMAs guided by human are able to recapture similar cell-proportions as the whole slide images (WSI)

Random TMAs had less concordance

Guided TMAs can recapture short distance cell-cell neighborhoods comparable to WSI

Next steps

Multiple parameter evaluations to created the guided and random TMAs in 22 whole slide images 

Selection of best parameters (core radius, number of cores) 

Evaluation of concordance to previously detected biological findings in Cancer cell paper

Evaluate core selection from H&E with matched tCycIF

Core selection by Pathologist in H&E
(Anni Virtanen)
